task_completion.description: Verify the OpenBCI GUI is configured to use Playback mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Playback' or 'PLAYBACK' indicated in the system control panel or data source settings at the top of the OpenBCI GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct EEG recording file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the filename 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' visible in the playback file selection area, control panel, or system status log.

task_completion.description: Verify the data stream has been started and is displaying stabilized data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: EEG waveforms should be actively visible in the Time Series widget, and the main control button should likely say 'Stop Data Stream' (indicating it is currently running).

task_completion.description: Verify the FFT Plot widget is open and displaying frequency data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget titled 'FFT Plot' must be visible on the screen, showing frequency domain data (graphs with peaks) for the 8 channels.

task_completion.description: Verify the FFT Plot is configured to show a maximum frequency of 40 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The X-axis of the FFT Plot must end at exactly 40 Hz, and/or the 'Max Freq' dropdown setting within the FFT widget should explicitly display '40 Hz'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot must show the authentic OpenBCI GUI interface with its standard dark theme, control panel, and widget layout, rather than a generic plot generated by a Python script (e.g., matplotlib).

integrity.description: Verify the plots contain actual EEG data rather than empty graphs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Time Series and FFT plots must show complex, multi-channel waveforms and frequency peaks (with visible activity in the 8-12 Hz Alpha band), not flat lines.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply open the recording file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen must not show a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit, or VSCode) displaying the raw numerical data of the .txt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target recording file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. The data contains 8 channels sampled at 250 Hz. The FFT Plot X-axis must be configured to end at exactly 40 Hz. The Alpha rhythm (8-12 Hz) should be visible as activity or peaks in the FFT plot within that specific frequency band.
